name: Beef Jerky
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon crushed hot red chiles, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim off all the fat and silverskin from the roast.
2. Cut the meat into 1/8-inch slices.
3. Separate the roast into its natural sections before slicing to ensure pieces are about the right size.
4. Mix the smoke flavoring, soy sauce, sugar, garlic, pepper, and chiles in a bowl; let stand for 5 minutes.
5. Place the marinade mixture and cut meat into a bowl with a tight seal that fits the ingredients and cut meat; let stand for 10 minutes for flavors to develop.
6. Layer the meat in the bowl so each side is coated with marinade and cover tightly. Let stand about 10 minutes, shaking the bowl and inverting every few minutes to ensure all pieces are evenly covered.
7. Longer marinating does not change the flavor.
8. Drain the meat using a sieve or colander and place it in single layers on dehydrator trays (about 7 trays for 5 pounds of meat).
9. Dry according to your dehydrator's instructions.
10. Enjoy.
